claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child (or children) you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age(s), and if you're claiming for multiple children, please tell me about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hello, thanks for helping me with this! I'm looking to claim for my two children. I've got Alex who's 5 years old, and Blake who's 10. So they're both quite young, under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! It's lovely to help you with this. Great news that both Alex (5) and Blake (10) are under 16 - that makes the first part straightforward!

Let me continue with the assessment for both children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
The next question is about whether the children are working. Given that Alex is 5 and Blake is 10, I expect they aren't in paid employment, but I need to confirm:

**Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in any paid work at all)?**

This would typically be "yes" for children their age, but please confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - neither of them work at all. They're just at school, so no paid work whatsoever.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means they are not personally claiming benefits such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit. This would be unusual for children their age, but I need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them gets any benefits in their own names. They're just kids, so nothing like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes sense! Let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake in any kind of apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (5 and 10), I'd expect the answer is no - they're too young for apprenticeships. But just to confirm, neither of them is enrolled in any apprenticeship programme, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not - they're far too young for that! They're just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, I thought as much! Just need to check all the boxes. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and your family
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Do any of these apply to you? For most people living in the UK, option 1 (normally lives in the UK) would apply.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, well actually... I don't live in the UK at the moment. We're living abroad right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see, thank you for letting me know. That's an important detail!

Living abroad doesn't automatically disqualify you, but we need to check if you fit into any of the special circumstances.

**Can I ask a bit more about your situation?**

1. **Are you a Crown servant?** (This includes diplomatic staff, armed forces personnel serving overseas, or civil servants posted abroad by the UK government)

2. **Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?** If so, are you:
   - Working and earning above the National Insurance threshold?
   - Actively seeking work?
   - A student with sufficient resources?
   - A family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside?

3. **Are you living in a qualifying country** with a special arrangement with the UK?

Could you tell me a bit more about why you're living abroad and your connection to the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. We're just living abroad - I don't have settled or pre-settled status, and I'm not in any of those special categories you mentioned. We're just living outside the UK as a regular family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being so clear about your situation. I'm afraid I have some disappointing news.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to say that based on what you've told me, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Summary

**Result: NOT ELIGIBLE**

### For Alex (age 5):
❌ **Not eligible** - While Alex meets all the child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), you unfortunately don't meet the UK residency requirements as the claimant.

### For Blake (age 10):
❌ **Not eligible** - Similarly, Blake meets all the child-related criteria, but the same residency issue applies.

---

### Why?
To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK, or meet special circumstances such as:
- Being a Crown servant posted abroad
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying conditions)
- Living in certain qualifying countries with special arrangements

Since you're living abroad as a regular family without any of these special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

---

### What you can do:
- **If you return to the UK** and become normally resident here, you would likely be eligible to claim Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake
- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., you become a Crown servant, or obtain settled status), you may become eligible
- You can find more information at: [gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex (age 5) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad, is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not fall into any other qualifying category for residency.

Blake (age 10) meets the child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad, is not a Crown servant, does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and does not fall into any other qualifying category for residency.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Both children (Alex, 5, and Blake, 10) would qualify as eligible children for Child Benefit purposes - they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in apprenticeships. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK, or meet specific criteria such as being a Crown servant abroad, having settled status, or having pre-settled status with qualifying circumstances. Since the claimant is living abroad as a regular family without any of these special circumstances, they cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
